name: Katy Independent School District
districtFact: Katy ISD spans three counties — Harris, Fort Bend, and Waller — across the western Houston suburbs, and is one of the primary receiving districts for energy-sector corporate relocations, with ExxonMobil, Shell, BP, and Chevron all maintaining major Houston-area campuses within commuting range of the district.

about.title: About Katy Independent School District
about.content: Katy ISD spans three counties — Harris, Fort Bend, and Waller — across the western Houston suburbs. The "Katy area" label is applied loosely in real estate and builder marketing; it covers a broad geography, and two households sharing the same zip code can feed into schools with very different campus cultures and track records. The established community of Cinco Ranch has long feeder patterns and predictable campus assignments, while newer developments like Cross Creek Ranch, Cane Island, and the outer Waller County corridors have seen repeated zone reassignments as the district opened campuses to absorb growth. School assignment is determined by attendance zone boundary, not city name or zip code, and those boundaries move. Verify your specific address at katyisd.org before relying on builder materials or any third-party school data.

about.title: What New Katy ISD Families Should Know
about.content: Katy ISD includes a Fall Break in October — a break that doesn't appear on Houston ISD, Fort Bend ISD, or Cypress-Fairbanks ISD calendars. For the 2026–2027 and 2027–2028 school years, Fall Break runs around Columbus Day weekend, giving students three school days off in mid-October plus the weekend, for five consecutive days away from campus. Households with children across multiple Houston-area districts, or parents whose work schedules follow a neighboring district's calendar, will need to plan separately for that week. The district runs 172 instructional days — fewer than most Texas districts — with extended daily class time to meet state requirements. Both the fall semester and the school year end with early dismissal.

content: Katy ISD includes a Fall Break in October that Houston ISD, Fort Bend ISD, and Cypress-Fairbanks ISD don't observe. Households with children in multiple Houston-area districts will need to manage around the offset, particularly for childcare arrangements in October.

The spring semester begins the first week of January, with a student holiday on the day immediately before the first day of classes. Build that single no-school day into post-holiday travel return plans.

Attendance zone boundaries in Katy ISD's newer growth corridors — Cross Creek Ranch, Cane Island, and the outer Waller County developments — have shifted as campuses opened. Verify your school assignment through the district's address lookup at katyisd.org; builder marketing materials in active construction areas frequently lag behind actual zone boundaries.

Spring Break and the Easter weekend are two separate breaks in Katy ISD, not the same week. In 2026–2027, Spring Break runs March 8–12 and Good Friday through Easter Monday falls March 26–29 — two full weeks later. Families booking spring travel or arranging childcare should mark both windows; programs that close only for Spring Break may still be open the week of Easter.

districtFaqs.q: Does Katy ISD have a Fall Break?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. Katy ISD schedules a Fall Break in October, typically running through or around Columbus Day weekend. For the 2026–2027 and 2027–2028 school years, Fall Break gives students three school days off in mid-October, extending the break to five or more consecutive days including the weekend. This is not standard among major suburban Houston districts — Houston ISD, Fort Bend ISD, and Cypress-Fairbanks ISD don't include a fall break. Parents relocating from those districts or from out of state should add it to their October planning calendar.

districtFaqs.q: How many instructional days does Katy ISD have?
districtFaqs.a: Katy ISD's calendar is built around 172 instructional days — fewer than the 175 or 180 days most Texas districts schedule. The district achieves state compliance through extended daily instructional minutes rather than a higher day count. The calendar is structured with enough minute-based buffer to absorb two weather-related closure days without requiring makeup days at the end of the year.

districtFaqs.q: When does the Katy ISD school year start and end?
districtFaqs.a: Katy ISD typically begins in early to mid-August and ends in late May. The fall semester ends with early dismissal in December before Winter Break; the spring semester begins the first week of January following a student holiday on the day before classes resume. Specific start and end dates vary by school year — the official KISD calendar at katyisd.org is the reliable source for current-year dates.

districtFaqs.q: Does Katy ISD have school on Veterans Day?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. Veterans Day (November 11) is not a school holiday in Katy ISD — it's a regular instructional day. The district schedules student holidays in October and early November, but those fall on different dates. If you're relocating from a state or district that observes Veterans Day as a no-school day, November 11 is one of the first calendar surprises — plan childcare for that date.

districtFaqs.q: When does the spring semester start in Katy ISD?
districtFaqs.a: Katy ISD students return for the spring semester in the first week of January. The Monday after Winter Break ends is consistently a student holiday — classes don't begin until Tuesday. Plan return-from-holiday travel to arrive home Sunday evening; the school bus runs Tuesday morning. This pattern has held across recent school years, though the exact calendar dates shift annually. Check the specific year's calendar on this page for the confirmed return date.

districtFaqs.q: Are Katy ISD students off on Good Friday?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. Katy ISD observes Good Friday and Easter Monday as student and staff holidays every year — the campus closes completely for both days. This is separate from Spring Break, which runs in mid-March; the Easter holiday falls four to six weeks later and the two breaks do not overlap. Unlike Spring Break (when some campus-based programs may still run), the Good Friday and Easter Monday closure applies to staff as well, so on-site before/after-school childcare doesn't operate on those days. Check the specific year's calendar for the exact April dates.

districtFaqs.q: Does Katy ISD have early release days?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. Katy ISD schedules early dismissal at the end of the fall semester and on the last day of the school year. The district may also include additional early release days for professional development throughout the year. Dismissal times on early release days vary by campus level — elementary and secondary campuses don't always release at the same hour. The official KISD calendar at katyisd.org lists all early release dates.

livingHere.intro: Katy ISD covers the western Houston suburbs — a corridor shaped by energy-sector employment, master-planned residential development, and rapid population growth across three counties. Two things catch out-of-area arrivals off guard: the Fall Break in October, which most major Texas districts don't observe, and the pace of attendance zone changes in newer subdivisions.

highlights.label: Energy Corridor and Corporate Relocations
highlights.detail:
The Houston Energy Corridor — the stretch along Interstate 10 between Beltway 8 and Highway 6 — is home to the US headquarters or major campuses of ExxonMobil, Shell, BP, Chevron, and dozens of oil and gas service companies. Much of the employee housing for that corridor falls within Katy ISD attendance zones. Mid-year enrollments driven by corporate relocations are routine at KISD campuses, not exceptional.

For households relocated to the Katy area by an energy company, timing matters. Corporate move dates are typically set by project start dates or lease transitions, not the school calendar. Initiating contact with the Katy ISD enrollment office before the physical move date — even a few weeks early — typically shortens the gap between arrival and the student's first school day. Required documentation is listed at katyisd.org.

At high schools in particular, mid-year arrivals in grades 10–12 are common enough that counselors are accustomed to evaluating credits from other Texas districts and from out-of-state systems. Credit evaluation timelines and placement decisions are handled at the campus level, so contacting the receiving high school directly — before the move — is more efficient than waiting until arrival.

highlights.label: Fall Break
highlights.detail: Katy ISD includes a Fall Break in October — a break that doesn't appear on Houston ISD, Fort Bend ISD, or Cypress-Fairbanks ISD calendars. For households with children across multiple Houston-area districts, or parents whose employers schedule around a neighboring district's calendar, the October offset creates a real coordination problem. The break runs around Columbus Day weekend, extending to five or more consecutive days off including the weekend.

highlights.label: Master-Planned Communities and Zone Assignment
highlights.detail:
The Cinco Ranch area — which spans Harris and Fort Bend County portions of Katy ISD — has established school feeder patterns and long campus track records at both the elementary and high school level. Cross Creek Ranch, Cane Island, and the newer developments in the Waller County corridor are a different situation. These areas have seen repeated zone reassignments as the district opened new campuses to manage enrollment growth. Households in recently built sections of these communities have found their actual school assignment different from what the builder's sales materials stated. The district's address lookup tool at katyisd.org reflects current zone assignments; materials in active construction phases often do not.

The tri-county nature of the district adds another layer. Harris County, Fort Bend County, and Waller County portions of Katy ISD all fall under the same district, but the communities, campus ages, and feeder patterns vary considerably across that geography.

highlights.label: August Heat
highlights.detail: School starts in early to mid-August — the peak of the Houston-area heat and humidity season. Daily heat indices regularly exceed 105°F through late August, and Houston's Gulf Coast humidity makes the felt temperature meaningfully higher than the air temperature. Outdoor PE schedules are modified during heat advisories, and after-school athletic practices in August typically run in early morning or late afternoon slots. Students arriving from the Pacific Coast or from less humid climates often find the first month of school more physically demanding than the temperatures alone suggest.

highlights.label: Weather Closures
highlights.detail: The greater Houston area faces two distinct weather disruption patterns: Gulf Coast tropical systems in late summer and early fall, and ice storms in January or February. Hurricane-related closures or early dismissals in August and September are uncommon in most years but do occur with short notice; the district monitors storm tracks and acts accordingly. Freezing weather in winter — less frequent than in North Texas but not rare — causes more consistent calendar disruption. Katy ISD builds buffer time into its calendar for weather days, and the 172-day structure with extended minute coverage provides additional flexibility.

compareIntro: Katy ISD is one of the few Houston-area districts that schedules a Fall Break in October. Families with children in multiple districts — or parents whose work schedule is tied to a neighboring district's calendar — should compare dates before booking travel or arranging childcare.

relatedDistricts.name: Houston Independent School District
relatedDistricts.reason: The largest district in Texas and the primary urban district adjacent to Katy ISD's eastern boundary; HISD does not observe Katy ISD's Fall Break, and Spring Break timing between the two districts varies year to year.

relatedDistricts.name: Fort Bend Independent School District
relatedDistricts.reason: Fort Bend ISD serves territory immediately south of Katy ISD's Fort Bend County portions; households near the boundary between the two districts should confirm which district their specific address falls in.

relatedDistricts.name: Cypress-Fairbanks Independent School District
relatedDistricts.reason: Cypress-Fairbanks ISD serves the northern Houston suburbs adjacent to Katy ISD; their overall calendar structure is similar, but CFISD does not include a Fall Break.

sources.label: NCES — Common Core of Data, Katy ISD
